resources.string: Keeps Overdrive vehicle monitoring active in the background. This service does not read or interact with screen content.
Routes MQTT through Tailscale so you can reach a private broker on your tailnet without port forwarding.
While enabled, MQTT to public brokers (HiveMQ, AWS IoT, etc.) will fail — Tailscale only routes to your tailnet. Saving will restart the daemon.
Pick the path that matches your head unit. Default fits most BYD models; DiLink 4 is required on newer trims (Han / Tang 2024+) where the AVM HAL emits a pre-stitched 2x2 mosaic.
Default — works on Seal, Atto, Dolphin (most cars)
Changing the mode restarts the camera daemon. If video looks wrong (squished, missing quadrants, red overlay), try the other mode.
Per-vehicle adjustments for the DiLink 4 path. Only effective when DiLink 4 mode is selected above.
Toggle these only if the HAL paints a red \"calibration failed\" chrome over the feed. Daemon restart applies the change.

Adds a fragment-shader pass that detects bright-red HAL \"calibration failed\" pixels and replaces them with a neighbour sample (or luminance gray when the banner is too large to inpaint). Cosmetic only — does not affect the underlying calibration state.
Auto picks the right camera for your trim on every boot. Camera 1 = BYD Seal, Camera 0 = Atto trims. Restart the daemon after changing camera ID for the setting to take effect.

Pick the AVMCamera id that points at the forward dashcam sensor. Dashcam is the OPPOSITE of pano: Seal/Han pano = 1 → dashcam = 0; Atto / Tang pano = 0 → dashcam = 1. Leave on Auto to let OverDrive pick.
Auto — pano id is %1$d, can\'t infer dashcam
Test dual-camera support on next boot

One-time check that opens both cameras at once to confirm this vehicle can run pano + dashcam concurrently. Runs once on the next restart while parked. Leave OFF unless recordings look wrong — it is not required and may briefly interrupt a recording if one is active when it runs.
Preview unavailable yet. Try another candidate or refresh by reopening the dialog after the daemon restarts.

OverDrive keeps the legacy startup behavior by default, but now lets you calibrate the merged panoramic strip properly. Preview each raw merged slice, map the logical 360 roles to the slice that actually shows front/right/rear/left on your vehicle, and the daemon will reuse that layout for recording, streaming, and AI after restart.
Three quick steps to get the best experience:
Defaults to your head unit\'s language. Tap to choose a different one for the OverDrive app and the web tunnel.
Tap below to open BYD Auto-Start. Find OverDrive in the list and uncheck the box. BYD wipes this on every install — you\'ll redo it after updates.
Enable this to show a floating status indicator for recording and trip tracking on top of other apps.
Overdrive runs your cameras through a small on-device helper. Android will ask once to allow USB debugging — tap Allow and check Always allow from this device so it stays on after every restart.
Accept the Android popup. If you don\'t see it, it may already be granted — this will continue on its own.
The helper isn\'t running yet. The Android permission may have been dismissed. Try again and remember to check Always allow.
Let\'s tell Overdrive which camera is your 360 view and which is your dashcam. It takes about a minute, and you can change it later.
Loading a live preview from your cameras. This can take a few seconds.
Use the arrows to find the stitched 4-up image. Read it from the BOTTOM: bottom-left is the REAR, bottom-right is the LEFT side.
Step through the cameras with the arrows and stop on the one showing the windshield / forward view. Note its number — you\'ll pick it next.
In the top card, choose the number for your 360 / normal camera. Not sure? Leave it on AUTO and Overdrive figures it out.
In this card, choose the dashcam number you found earlier — or leave it on AUTO.
The feed will go dark and blink for about 10 seconds. This is normal — hang tight.
Does the 360 view show the stitched image and the dashcam show the road ahead? If a feed looks wrong, tap Re-pick — nothing is broken.
For the OEM dashcam layout — the windshield camera as a strip on top of your recording — pick Windshield in the role menu, choose the forward camera, and Save. Most owners can skip this.
Pick your model and we fill in the kWh, or type it yourself (15–120). This makes range and charging estimates accurate. You can skip and set it later.
These cards are buttons — tap to open Live cameras, Recordings, Services or your Vehicle.
The chips up top show recording, services and your remote link. They update on their own — they\'re not buttons.
Your live status — recording, services and remote link — shows up here. It updates on its own.
Cameras mapped and battery set. Tap Live to watch your feeds — or explore the deeper settings any time.
A quick walk through the deeper setup — your vehicle profile, integrations, surveillance, diagnostics, services and security. You can stop any time.
Tap the Vehicle tile to set your battery size and model — it powers accurate range and health estimates.
Switch between Dashcam (continuous + proximity clips) and Surveillance (parked sentry events) with this toggle — each keeps its own library. Tap a clip to play it.
Open Live from here to watch all cameras in real time. Handy for checking the view before a drive or while parked nearby.
Trips logs every drive — route, distance, duration and energy use — so you can review where the car has been.

RoadSense uses the car\'s motion sensors and GPS to detect road hazards — bumps, potholes, speed breakers — and warns you before known ones. Its tabs cover detection, the hazard map, warning chimes, and Blind Spot. Turn on the master switch to use any of it.

Enable Blind Spot in the tab here and, when you signal a turn, a side-camera view appears on screen automatically — left for a left signal, right for a right one — then hides shortly after. It only runs while a turn signal is on.

Open the Map for full turn-by-turn navigation — search a destination, get routing and live guidance, plus nearby points of interest and any road hazards RoadSense has logged along the way. You can also auto-project it onto the instrument cluster when you start driving — that toggle lives in RoadSense\'s Map tab.
Connect Telegram alerts, A Better Routeplanner, MQTT or BYD Cloud. Each card opens its setup inside the app. The pill up top turns green when everything\'s linked.
Linking your BYD account here enables remote lock, flash and horn from your phone. Treat the linked account and access code as sensitive.
Fine-tune detection, quality and storage here. These open as in-app config pages served from your car — a blank screen just means the helper is still starting.
This re-maps which physical camera feeds each role and restarts the cameras. Only change it if a feed looks wrong — you set this up earlier in the guide.
Traffic monitor — required for remote access

BYD\'s built-in Traffic Monitor blocks other apps from using the car\'s built-in SIM. If you want to reach your car remotely over the BYD SIM, you must disable it here. The head unit reboots to apply the change.
These switches start and stop background services. Turning OFF Camera, Surveillance or ACC Sentry stops recording and keeps them off until you turn them back on — even after a restart.
Set a PIN to gate the app for shared vehicles. Surveillance and recording keep running while the app is locked. A forgotten PIN can only be cleared from a computer over USB.
This erases recordings, events and settings and can\'t be undone. It\'s here if you ever sell the car — not part of normal use.
You\'ve seen the deeper settings. Open this guide again any time from the help button.
Run shell commands on the device
Start the Camera Daemon from the Daemons screen to access this page.
Token is stored securely and shared between app and daemon processes.
Removes environment and token. You will need to re-enable with your token (uses a device slot).
This cannot be undone. Recordings, trips, and battery history will be permanently deleted.
SoC samples, charging sessions, voltage logs
Re-detects nominal capacity, re-seeds estimate from BMS
Username, login key, VIN — re-login required
All MP4s in the recordings folder
Surveillance event clips and JSON sidecars
Try adjusting your date range or chip filters.
Captures will appear here as soon as the dashcam or surveillance pipeline writes a clip.
One moment while the daemon scans your recordings.
Clears all data and re-estimates from scratch. Use if battery was replaced or reading seems incorrect.

Vehicle reports %1$.1f kWh at full charge but pack capacity is set to %2$.1f kWh. The number above may reflect a unit mismatch (nameplate vs usable), not real degradation. Consider entering %1$.1f kWh to match what the vehicle reports.

plurals.name: delete_recordings_message
plurals.item: This will permanently delete %d recording. This cannot be undone.
This will permanently delete %d recordings. This cannot be undone.

resources.string: A newer build is ready to install
Install any published build — newer or older
Camera daemon isn\'t running. Start it from the Daemons screen, then try updating again.
Lost contact with the head unit before the install finished. On a tunnel? Check Telegram for the new URL, or use the local IP.
The stable release. Every published version is kept — pick any to install (downgrades allowed). Recommended for everyday use.

Bleeding-edge builds, delivered the moment they\'re published. You get new features first, but they may be unstable or buggy. If something breaks, open the Daemons screen, tap a daemon\'s log, choose \"Upload & get a code\", and share the code with us on the community channels.
Could not save the update channel
Switched to Braveheart. Builds may be unstable — if something breaks, send the daemon log and report it on Discord/GitHub/WhatsApp.
Camera daemon restarting with full probe
Config cleared but daemon restart failed. Please restart manually.
Camera settings saved — restarting camera daemon…
Camera settings were saved, but daemon restart failed. Please restart it manually.
SOH estimation reset — will recalculate from next data
Reset failed — daemon not responding and file not writable

This will clear all SOH data and force re-estimation from scratch.

Use this if:
• Battery was replaced
• SOH reading seems incorrect
• You want to recalibrate

The system will re-seed from the next available data source (OEM, charge calibration, or instantaneous reading).

ADB connection is not ready yet.

The app needs ADB access to manage system packages. Please ensure:

• USB Debugging is enabled in Developer Options
• The ADB authorization prompt has been accepted

The status will update automatically once connected.

The BYD Traffic Monitor (com.byd.trafficmonitor) is a built-in system app that continuously monitors road traffic conditions in the background.

Why disable it?

• Consumes mobile data (even when parked)
• Uses CPU and battery in the background
• Not needed if you use a separate navigation app
• Can interfere with the dashcam's network usage

This is safe to disable — it only affects the built-in traffic overlay on the map. Your navigation, Bluetooth, and all other car functions remain unaffected.

A hard reboot is required after disabling (hold center console button 5 seconds).

The BYD Traffic Monitor is currently disabled.

Re-enabling it will restore the built-in traffic overlay on the navigation map. Note that it will run in the background and consume mobile data.

A hard reboot is required after enabling (hold center console button 5 seconds).

The change has been applied.

Please perform a hard reboot now:
Press and hold the central console button for 5 seconds.
This will invalidate the current token. All active sessions will be logged out. Continue?
New token generated. All sessions logged out.
Token regenerated. Daemon may need restart to apply.
Token regenerated. Could not notify daemon.
Enter your Zrok enable token.\nGet one at: zrok.io
Logged in. Click to login to a different account

This routes MQTT through Tailscale so you can reach a private broker on your tailnet without port forwarding.

While enabled:
• MQTT to a tailnet broker works (e.g. Mosquitto on a device on your tailnet)
• MQTT to public brokers (HiveMQ, AWS IoT, etc.) will fail — Tailscale only routes to your tailnet
• The Tailscale daemon will restart to apply the change
• Other app traffic and the rest of the device are not affected

You can turn this off anytime.

This will:
• Stop the zrok tunnel if running
• Remove the zrok environment from this device
• Delete the saved token

You will need to re-enter your token and re-enable. This uses one of your 5 device slots on zrok.io.

Are you sure?
Zrok environment reset. Enter a new token to set up again.
Environment reset (token file may need manual cleanup)
No token configured. Tap to set up.

This will:
• Stop the tailscale tunnel if running
• Remove the tailscale environment from this device
• You will still need to remove the device from the tailscale console

You will need to log in again.

Are you sure?
Tailscale environment reset. Login to set up again.
Log file is empty or not found
Daemon manager not ready, try again in a moment
Log file not found or unreadable
NOTE: Log truncated to last 10000 lines (total: %1$d lines)
%1$s · choose how to send it to us
Sends the log to us and gives you a short code. Easiest for support.
Save or send the log yourself via the share sheet.
Share this code with us, along with a note on what you were doing when it broke. Post it on Discord, GitHub Issues or WhatsApp — links are on the About screen.
Updated to %1$s — re-confirm autostart, BYD wipes it on every install
The BYD Traffic Monitor blocks third-party apps from reaching the internet. Disabling it lets OverDrive and any other apps you install actually use the network. A hard reboot is required afterwards.
Open Diagnostics → Camera probe, set Camera ID to 0, tap Save, then restart the camera daemon. If the feeds are still wrong, repeat with Camera ID 1. Leave the mapping section untouched.
No estimate yet — waiting for data
Choose which segments of the floating status pill stay visible.
Show the REC / PROX badge while recording is active.
Show the TRIP badge while trip detection is running.
Reset clears recordings index, cached credentials, daemon state, and on-device preferences. The action cannot be undone.
Check the latest GitHub release and install if newer.
Save a backup file of your current settings. Keep it private — it holds your credentials.
Load a backup file made on this same head unit.
Could not save the backup file
No backup files found. Export one first, or copy a backup .json into %1$s, then try again.
That file is not a valid Overdrive backup
The camera service must be running to back up or restore. Start it from Daemons, then try again.
This replaces your current Overdrive settings with the backup and reloads services. It only works on the same head unit the backup was made on.
Settings restored. Restart the camera daemon (Daemons → Camera → Restart) to make sure every restored setting is applied.
Include your trip history (distance, energy, cost, scores, start/end locations)? Detailed per-trip telemetry is not included. The file will contain your location history — keep it private.
Every shared link helps another BYD owner discover Overdrive.
Check out Overdrive — open-source surveillance & dashcam for BYD: https://overdrive-5lc.pages.dev/
A coffee on Ko-fi keeps the late-night commits coming.
MIT — open source. Tap to view full text.
Takes a second. Means a lot.
Built with the help of contributors and supporters.
List populates as people pitch in.
Tap any item on the left to play it.
Live health, logs, and probes for the device.
Inspect cell SOH and pack stats.
Send drive summaries, motion alerts, and SOS notifications to your private Telegram bot.
Push live battery and route data to A Better Routeplanner for charging predictions.
Publish vehicle telemetry to Home Assistant or any MQTT broker.
Connect your BYD app account to enable remote commands like door lock, flash lights, and horn.
15 to 120 kWh. Leave to use the model default.
Tune appearance, recording, surveillance, and on-device data.
Clear recordings, events, or all caches.
OverDrive runs entirely on the head unit. No telemetry leaves your car except via the tunnels and integrations you explicitly configure.
Choose categories: recordings, events, daemon configs, cached telemetry…
Lock the OverDrive app so passengers and valets can\'t open it without your PIN.
Surveillance, recording, deterrent, alerts, and background services keep running while the app is locked. The PIN gates the OverDrive interface, nothing else.
On — PIN required to open OverDrive.
Confirm current PIN, then set a new one.
Forgotten PIN can be cleared from a host machine over USB debugging without a factory reset. Run this command, then reopen the app.
Pick a 4–8 digit PIN. You\'ll need this every time you open the app.
Confirm your current PIN to turn off the app lock.
Enter your current PIN, then you\'ll set a new one.
Too many attempts. Try again in a moment.
Couldn\'t save the PIN. Try again.
PIN set. App lock is on.
Too many attempts. Try again shortly.
Forgot your PIN? Run from a connected computer:\n%1$s
Welcome — Overdrive is now your second pair of eyes.
Could not reach RoadSense. Try again.
Couldn\'t reach the search service. Check your connection or proxy and try again.
Could not find a route. Try again.
Add a routing key in RoadSense settings to navigate.
Routing key rejected. Check your key in RoadSense settings.
Network too slow to find a route. Check your connection and try again.
Routing service error (%1$d). Try again shortly.
Search a place to set as %1$s
Token is stored encrypted and shared between the app and daemon processes.
Define whether you are using paid Cloudflare or trycloudflare. If you are using paid Cloudflare, enter your tunnel token.
If you set the paid version the token cannot be empty
Invalid token: use only letters, numbers and . _ - = characters
